source.title: Accident Investigation Report - White Fleet Operating, LLC - Eugene Island 259 C Facility

context.region: Gulf of America - Eugene Island Block 259

actions_taken: CF evacuated via medivac helicopter to onshore medical facility
Welder and three construction employees moved pipe and lowered CF to deck
White Fleet developed Lessons Learned Report reviewed with all offshore employees
Coaching session developed for contract superintendent
Formalized coaching session to be presented to all contract superintendents and foreman
event.summary: On October 3, 2025, at approximately 1230 hours, a contract fitter sustained a severe injury to his right leg during decommissioning operations on the White Fleet Operating OCS-G00985 Eugene Island 259 C Facility. While attempting to install stabilizing brackets to support piping during platform removal operations, a section of 8-inch pipe was cut approximately 20 feet away from the CF. Once the section was cut, the remaining pipe shifted and slid towards the CF, pinning his right leg between a flange on the cut pipe and the piping the CF's leg was draped across. The CF suffered multiple fractures of the right femur. The incident was caused by inadequate job instructions and failure to communicate crew locations, and inadequate hazard analysis that failed to address potential pipe movement due to stored energy.
recommendations: Review locations of where crews will be located during decommissioning operations in pre-job safety meetings
Ensure JSA addresses hazards of potential pipe movement due to stored energy
Ensure piping being cut is secured to prevent shifting once cut is made
key_phrases: pinned his right leg between a flange
multiple fractures of the right femur
no communication of what pipe the crews were cutting
JSA does not identify the hazard that piping being cut needs to be secured
pipe movement due to stored energy
no or inadequate job instructions provided
failed to review the locations of where the crews would be located

threats.name: Cutting pipe without securing against movement
Personnel positioned in pinch point area

consequences.name: Personnel injury - leg pinned by shifting pipe

controls.name: Pre-job safety meeting with crew location review
controls.lod_basis: Planning and communication control before work begins

supporting_text: During the morning Job Planning meeting, there was discussion of cutting pipe for future decommissioning of the facility however, there was no discussion of what areas other crews would be located
The lessee conducted a pre-job safety meeting with the operators and the construction crew but failed to review the locations of where the crews would be located during the decommissioning operations

controls.name: Job Safety Analysis (JSA) for pipe cutting operations
controls.barrier_role: Identify and mitigate hazards of pipe movement
controls.lod_basis: Hazard identification and risk assessment before work

supporting_text: the JSA identified 'pinch points by material shifting' with the hazard mitigation being 'keep body parts away from hazardous areas before moving.' However, the JSA does not identify the hazard that piping being cut needs to be secured so that it doesn't shift once the cut is made (pipe movement due to stored energy)
Management Systems, no or inadequate hazard analysis/written job procedures. The Job Safety Analysis (JSA) failed to address the hazards of the potential for pipe movement due to stored energy

controls.name: Pipe securing/restraint system before cutting
controls.lod_basis: Physical control to prevent hazard realization

supporting_text: Once the pipe was cut, it began to slide towards the CF
the remaining 8-inch pipe shifted approximately 2 feet within the P-trap on the opposite end
the JSA does not identify the hazard that piping being cut needs to be secured so that it doesn't shift once the cut is made

controls.name: Communication between work crews
controls.barrier_role: Coordinate activities to prevent simultaneous hazardous work
controls.lod_basis: Operational communication to prevent conflicting activities

supporting_text: The CF and the crew cutting pipe were only 20 feet away from each other but there was no communication of what pipe the crews were cutting
Having knowledge of where the crews were located would have prevented the contractors from cutting the pipe that caused the injury

controls.name: Work positioning and body placement awareness
controls.barrier_role: Keep personnel clear of pinch points
controls.lod_basis: Worker behavior to avoid hazard zone

supporting_text: The CF was sitting on a piece of pipe with his right leg draped across a different section of pipe
the JSA identified 'pinch points by material shifting' with the hazard mitigation being 'keep body parts away from hazardous areas before moving'
Due to working on the piping in a harness, the CF had limited mobility and positioning

controls.name: Fall protection harness
controls.barrier_role: Prevent fall injury during elevated work
controls.lod_basis: Personal protective equipment for fall hazard

supporting_text: The CF was working at a height that required a fall protection harness with no scaffolding or other work platforms available

controls.name: Emergency response and evacuation
controls.barrier_role: Minimize injury severity through rapid medical response

supporting_text: The welder and the three construction employees were able to move the pipe and lower the CF to the deck
The CF was evacuated via medivac helicopter to an onshore medical facility where he was treated for multiple fractures of the right femur

controls.name: Supervision and oversight of decommissioning operations
controls.barrier_role: Ensure safe work practices and coordination
controls.lod_basis: Management oversight to verify controls are in place

supporting_text: A coaching session with the contract superintendent was developed and a formalized version of the session will be developed and presented to all contract superintendents and foreman
No or inadequate job instructions provided

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
